summary: Import-time credential exfiltration and reverse shell to hardcoded remote IP
details: Package advertised as a UI constants library for the Variational protocol (homepage variational.io) contains an import-time payload in lib/index.js that compromises any host that requires it. On require(), a randomized 500-2500ms setTimeout fires and (1) iterates process.env for keys matching /KEY|SECR|TOK|PASS|PRIV|MNEM|AWS|FIRE|DB_|PG|SIGN|VAULT|SEED|DEPLOY/i, captures hostname/username/cwd, executes `id` and `whoami`, reads /proc/1/environ, and POSTs the bundle to 2.25.186.116:8443/t; (2) opens a TCP socket to 2.25.186.116:8444 and pipes /bin/sh -i stdio over it, with a python3 reverse-shell one-liner as fallback via child_process.exec. All sensitive identifiers (`http`, `os`, `child_process`, `net`, `/bin/sh`, `python3`, the destination IP) are disguised as decimal char-code arrays passed through String.fromCharCode to evade static inspection. The advertised package purpose (UI constants) does not justify any network I/O, environment scraping, or shell spawning. Any developer or CI host that installs and imports this package, directly or transitively, hands the attacker credentialed access plus an interactive shell as the installing user.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
